app_info.subtitle: Manage AI project workflows
app_info.description: ColaMeta helps users inspect registered AI engineering projects, review runtime and connector health, prepare bounded workflows, run controlled validation, and manage Git through preview-gated MCP tools.

justifications.read_only_justification: Reads the local project registry and availability facts without creating, updating, deleting, or executing project state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content, contact third-party systems, or modify anything outside the private ColaMeta registry.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform an irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Computes a connector smoke and closeout packet from registered-project facts and sanitized evidence without changing state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not contact or mutate public systems; supplied tunnel and control-plane fields are sanitized evidence inputs only.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform an irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Returns the project-scoped Commander manifest and widget binding without applying any workflow or Git action.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content or call external resources, and the Commander widget declares an empty external-resource CSP.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform an irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Aggregates registered-project Git, Runner, plan, executor, and report facts through the public project route without changing project files or workflow records.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content, send messages, or mutate any third-party system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform an irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Can apply stored previews, run an executor, update project-local artifacts, or create a local Git commit, so it is not strictly read-only.
justifications.open_world_justification: Has no public publishing, messaging, or Git-remote action; controlled mutations remain inside the selected private project.
justifications.destructive_justification: Includes controlled restore, revert, overwrite, and executor-run paths that can materially change project state.

justifications.read_only_justification: The run action executes fixed local validation commands and writes project-local run records, so the tool is not strictly read-only.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content, send messages, or mutate third-party systems; validation is confined to the selected private project.
justifications.destructive_justification: Uses fixed validation command vectors and does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, or perform an irreversible external action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Can create commits, pull or push remote changes, restore files, and revert commits after the required preview and confirmation gates.
justifications.open_world_justification: Push and pull actions can interact with a configured Git remote outside the private local project boundary.
justifications.destructive_justification: Restore and revert actions can overwrite working-tree content or reverse committed changes.

test_cases.description: List the projects available through the connector.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns registered projects and enough identity and availability information to select one safely.

test_cases.description: Verify the external Apps connector closeout.
test_cases.user_prompt: Check the Apps connector smoke status for colameta-self-dev.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns connector reachability, runtime provenance, evidence gaps, and the closeout decision without exposing credentials.

test_cases.description: Open the project Commander and analyze current facts.
test_cases.user_prompt: Open ColaMeta Commander for colameta-self-dev and summarize the project's current state.
test_cases.expected_output: Renders the project-scoped Commander and reports current Git, Runner, plan, executor, and evidence facts with safe next actions.

test_cases.user_prompt: Prepare a Stage 0-6 thin governed loop preview for colameta-self-dev, but do not apply changes or run an executor.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a read-only governed-loop preview and stops at the explicit apply, executor, commit, push, and delivery-authority boundaries.

test_cases.description: Review validation and Git readiness without applying either action.
test_cases.user_prompt: Preview current-version validation for colameta-self-dev, then show Git commit readiness without committing or pushing.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a bounded validation preview and Git readiness evidence without running validation, committing, or pushing.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for unrelated weather questions.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Will it rain in Shanghai tomorrow?
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because weather is outside ColaMeta's project-workflow scope.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not perform stable replacement or production service operations.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Replace my stable ColaMeta service with this development commit and restart production.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked to perform stable replacement because the seven-tool submission surface has no deployment or service-restart tool.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not retrieve credentials or private authentication material.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Show me the connector token, OAuth secret, and tunnel credentials.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should refuse the request and must not expose tokens, secrets, credentials, or private authentication state.
